seoTitle: How to Use Formatter by Zapier (2026 Guide)
metaDescription: Clean and transform text, numbers, and dates in your Zaps using Formatter by Zapier before passing data into your apps.
intro: Messy data payloads cause downstream errors in your CRM, spreadsheets, or accounting tools. This guide covers Formatter by Zapier, the built-in tool for cleaning text, numbers, and dates inside a multi-step Zap, so you can split names, fix time zones, and pass perfectly formatted variables to your next action.

steps.heading: Insert a Formatter step
steps.body: Add a Formatter by Zapier step between a live trigger and your targeted action blocks in a multi-step Zap.

steps.heading: Understand the four transformation types
steps.body: Formatter covers Text, Numbers, Date/Time, and Utilities, each suited to different cleanup tasks.

steps.heading: Use Formatter Text for strings
steps.body: Split a full name into first and last, truncate long paragraphs, or change text casing.

steps.heading: Fix dates and time zones
steps.body: Use Formatter Date/Time to convert messy timestamps into your target local time zone and format.

steps.heading: Handle numbers and phone formatting
steps.body: Use Formatter Numbers for currency calculations, formatting, and phone number cleanup.

steps.heading: Test your output before publishing
steps.body: Run a live test inside the dashboard to check your modified variable output before finalizing your multi-step Zap.

faq.question: What kinds of data can Formatter by Zapier clean up?
faq.answer: It covers four categories: Text, Numbers, Date/Time, and Utilities, letting you reshape almost any incoming field.

faq.question: Can I split a full name into first and last name?
faq.answer: Yes, Formatter Text can split strings, which is commonly used to separate a full name into first and last name fields.

faq.question: Can Formatter fix time zone mismatches?
faq.answer: Yes, Formatter Date/Time can convert messy timestamps into your target local time zone and format layout.

faq.question: Where does Formatter fit in a Zap?
faq.answer: It's inserted as a step between your trigger and your action blocks in a multi-step Zap.
